docs.title: About Us
docs.text:
Transforming Data into Insightful Action

Welcome to Infera Group LLC, a data science and analytics consultancy founded by James Eells, PhD. We specialize in applying predictive modeling, econometrics, and data visualization to solve complex business problems and drive measurable impact.

docs.title: 👋 About James Eells
docs.text:
I'm a data science leader with over two decades of experience in financial services, higher education, and social impact domains. My work blends rigorous statistical modeling with practical business insight to help organizations make smarter, faster decisions.

I hold a PhD in Economics from Oklahoma State University and have led high-impact analytics initiatives at Ameriprise Financial, American Express, and Oklahoma State University. My approach emphasizes transparency, reproducibility, and alignment with stakeholder goals.

docs.title: 🛠️ Tools & Technologies
docs.text:
• Languages: Python, R, SQL, SAS
  • Visualization: Power BI, Tableau, Shiny
  • Cloud & MLOps: AWS (SageMaker, Glue, Athena), Azure
  • Modeling: Regression (All Flavors), LSTM, Causal Inference, Time Series
  • Governance: SR 11-7 Compliance, Model Risk Management

docs.title: 🔍 Services
docs.text:
At Infera Group, we help clients:

  • Design and validate predictive models
  • Measure program impact using causal inference
  • Optimize marketing and service strategies
  • Build interactive dashboards and data pipelines
  • Modernize analytics ecosystems (e.g., cloud migration, MLOps)
  • Ensure compliance with model governance standards (e.g., SR 11-7)

We believe small, agile teams can deliver big results—especially when analytics is embedded directly into decision-making.

docs.title: 📈 Analytics Case Studies
docs.text: Optimizing decisions through rigorous modeling, measurement, and visualization.

docs.title: 🎓 OSU Entry-Level Placement Assessment (ELPA)
docs.text:
Client Context OSU’s legacy placement methods leaned heavily on standardized tests, creating inequities and excess remediation.

Approach Introduced the Predicted Grade Index (PGI) using regression models with high school GPA, class rank, and test scores. Embedded in enrollment workflows and recalibrated routinely.

Impact Transformed placement accuracy, assessing over 4,400 students annually with remediation rates below 5%. Adapted to students without test scores to extend equity.

docs.title: 📊 Key Driver Analysis for Program ROI Measurement
docs.text:
Client Context Post-financial crisis, 75 programs needed standardized ROI metrics to justify funding.

Approach Built panel data regression with advisor, client, and market controls. Defined “net flows” and branded as Key Driver Analysis (KDA).

Impact Enabled program comparisons and capital allocation based on empirical value. Became the core evaluation framework across lines of business.

docs.title: 💼 Measuring Wholesaler Impact on Financial Outcomes
docs.text:
Client Context Firm leadership lacked visibility into how wholesalers affected net flows and product sales.

Approach Created dynamic panel regression with DAGs and zero-inflated Poisson models. Reproduced in Python for cloud deployment.

Impact Attributed 22% of net flows to wholesaler engagement. Validated team ROI and guided resource allocation.

docs.title: 📊 Transforming Net Flow Reporting with Dashboards
docs.text:
Client Context Static reporting delayed insight generation and hindered exploratory analysis.

Approach Built Tableau, Power BI (with Sankey), and Plotly dashboards, integrating data dimensions for geography, advisor, and time.

Impact Reduced ad hoc analysis demand. Empowered real-time exploration and strategic decision-making across the enterprise.

🔙 Back to Case Study Index 🔙 Return to Homepage

docs.title: ⚖️ Compliance & Governance Case Studies
docs.text: Aligning analytics with risk, regulation, and responsible oversight.

docs.title: 🔍 Predictive Risk Modeling for Advisor Surveillance
docs.text:
Client Context Rule-based surveillance created high false positives and consumed analyst bandwidth.

Approach Modeled five risk behaviors using logistic regression. Created a General Risk Index based on probability × impact.

Impact Reduced false positives by 15%. Improved targeting and introduced a transparent, data-driven compliance layer.

docs.title: ☁️ Migrating Legacy Analytics to AWS Cloud
docs.text:
Client Context SAS-based ecosystem was unscalable and non-compliant with modern ML practices.

Approach Migrated datasets via Glue/Athena. Rebuilt models using SageMaker AutoML and scored through Batch Transform. Integrated fairness with SageMaker Clarify.

Impact Boosted scoring performance by 30%. Delivered modern MLOps compliance ahead of schedule and reduced operational costs.

docs.title: ✅ Achieving SR 11-7 Compliance for Model Governance
docs.text:
Client Context Federal Reserve review flagged non-compliance with SR 11-7 across model lifecycle.

Approach Led a 12-month remediation program with gated milestones, collaborative remediation, and embedded lifecycle uplift.

Impact Achieved full compliance on schedule. Raised governance maturity and restored regulator confidence.

🔙 Back to Case Study Index 🔙 Return to Homepage

docs.title: 🤝 Customer Experience Case Studies
docs.text: Enriching service delivery through intelligent prioritization and engagement.

docs.title: ⚖️ Real-Time Optimization of Dispute Resolution
docs.text:
Client Context Manual, high-cost billing disputes slowed resolution and harmed satisfaction.

Approach Built a real-time expert system using predictive modeling and economic logic. Deployed in SAS on mainframe for seamless call-center integration.

Impact Reduced resolution time by 90%, cut costs by 30%, and increased customer satisfaction across the board.

docs.title: 💬 Early NLP for Customer Service Prioritization
docs.text:
Client Context Rep notes contained valuable urgency cues but lacked scalable processing.

Approach Used keyword extraction and sentiment rules to create escalation indicators. Embedded into triage models.

Impact Enabled targeted routing of priority cases. Proved the value of unstructured data in service ops.

docs.title: 💡 Lifetime Value Modeling for Service Differentiation
docs.text:
Client Context Static segmentation limited proactive service across customers and merchants.

Approach Built predictive LTV and suppression risk models using transaction and cost data. Deployed for batch-based prioritization.

Impact Improved identification of high-value accounts and reduced churn by 6%. Lowered servicing costs by 7%.

docs.title: 📬 Optimizing Lead Generation for Financial Advisors
docs.text:
Client Context Over-reliance on massive mail campaigns strained budgets and ROI.

Approach Modeled response/conversion for RFI/RFC leads and ranked prospects by efficiency score.

Impact Met lead demand at half the original volume. Improved campaign cost-efficiency and advisor satisfaction.

docs.title: 🧪 Accelerating Marketing Insights with Multivariable Testing
docs.text:
Client Context Legacy testing lacked nuance and masked variable interactions.

Approach Used Plackett-Burman followed by Resolution IV factorial designs to uncover main and interaction effects.

Impact Streamlined testing and uncovered high-impact creatives. Established a scalable testing standard.

docs.title: 🎯 Advisor Focus List for Targeted Engagement
docs.text:
Client Context Scaling outreach demanded a more intelligent approach to advisor prioritization.

Approach Used LightGBM to model sales propensity. Created the Advisor Focus List with dual-level segmentation and evaluation via score matching.

Impact Focus advisors produced 40% more sales. Became cornerstone of wholesaler strategy.

docs.title: 📈 Modeling Optimal Contact Strategy Using LSTM
docs.text:
Client Context Leadership suspected contact sequence optimization could improve sales.

Approach Used LSTM to model time-dependent advisor responses. Included contact type and cadence features.

Impact Identified 5–8 contact sweet spot and flagged suboptimal sequences. Refined wholesaler outreach strategy.

docs.title: 🔄 Quantifying Advisor Response to Investment Recommendations
docs.text:
Client Context Research group sought behavioral validation for quarterly signals.

Approach Built time-series models aligning advisor trades/net flows to rec timelines. Modeled decay effects.

Impact Buy recommendations increased trading volume by 50%, sell by 30%. Validated research ROI and influence.

docs.title: 🧬 Validating Retirement Recommendations with Synthetic Data
docs.text:
Client Context Retiree data lacked diversity for stress-testing proprietary models.

Approach Used SMOTE and noise-injected sampling to generate 80M synthetic profiles. Ran benchmark and proprietary models across population.

Impact Confirmed model alignment with fiduciary goals. Strengthened go-to-market confidence and model fairness.

docs.text: Causal Inference & Data Science Consultant Strategic Modeling for Measurable Impact SAS | Python | R | Power BI | Tableau | AWS | Azure

docs.title: Ameriprise Financial, Phoenix, AZ
docs.text:
Vice President, Data Science 2005 – 2024

  • Led cross-functional teams designing ML pipelines and measurement frameworks to assess program impact and operational performance.
  • Developed econometric models to quantify incremental lift in enterprise net flows, supporting strategic decision-making.
  • Created interactive Power BI dashboards to visualize fund flows and program outcomes, enhancing stakeholder understanding.
  • Served on enterprise-wide governance committees to ensure compliance with model risk standards.

Senior Director, Data Science

  • Designed and implemented key driver analysis models to evaluate over 100 internal programs, directly informing executive strategy.
  • Built Tableau tools for senior leaders to explore program performance and identify areas for improvement.
  • Led A/B testing and experimental design for marketing and compliance initiatives, improving targeting and reducing risk.
  • Mentored data scientists and facilitated internal learning forums to promote data literacy and innovation.

docs.title: American Express, Phoenix, AZ
docs.text:
Director, Econometrics 1997 – 2005

  • Developed predictive models and economic logic for customer service and risk operations.
  • Applied NLP techniques to extract insights from qualitative data, improving dispute resolution strategies.
  • Estimated customer lifetime value using time series forecasting and multivariate analysis.
